title: Hot Garlic Sauce

ingredients.title: Ingredients (makes ~1 cup / 240 ml)
items: 3 tablespoons vegetable oil (or neutral oil like canola)
2–3 fresh red chilies, finely minced (adjust to heat preference)
2 tablespoons chili paste (optional, for deeper heat)
2 tablespoons soy sauce (light soy for color and umami)
1 tablespoon rice vinegar (or Chinese black vinegar)
¼ cup chicken or vegetable stock (or water)

instruction.title: Prepare aromatics
steps: Finely mince garlic and chilies for smooth texture and strong aroma.

instruction.title: Heat oil
steps: Heat oil in a wok or pan over medium-low heat.
Stir for 30–45 seconds until fragrant but not browned.

instruction.title: Add liquids
steps: Add soy sauce, vinegar, stock, and sugar.
Stir and bring to a gentle simmer.

instruction.title: Thicken sauce
steps: Mix cornstarch with water to make slurry.

instruction.title: Finish
steps: Adjust salt or sugar to taste.

instruction.title: Cool & store
steps: Use immediately or store in a sealed jar.
Keeps refrigerated for up to 1 week.

chefTips: Avoid browning garlic — it becomes bitter.
Mix fresh chilies + chili paste for better depth of heat.
Add MSG (optional) for restaurant-style umami boost.
Works great as stir-fry sauce or dumpling dip.
